message:
fix(agent): enforce RootDir boundary on write_file, edit_file, shell cwd

Previously resolve() returned absolute paths unchanged, so the write_file
and edit_file tools could write anywhere the OS allowed regardless of the
agent's working directory. ExecCommand accepted absolute workingDir paths
outside RootDir with no check. apply_patch was the only tool enforcing a
boundary.

The gap let a debug_fix agent edit the orchestrator toil's source tree
(definitions/workflows/verify.yaml, internal/engine/engine.go) from
inside a project worktree in run flint-oak-willow — edits that couldn't
take effect without a restart, causing the debugger loop to exhaust
max_loop_iterations.

Add resolveWrite() that cleans + absolutises + rejects paths outside
RootDir, wire it into WriteFile and EditFile. Reject non-descendant
workingDir in ExecCommand. Resolve symlinks on both sides of the
comparison via resolveSymlinksBestEffort so macOS /var -> /private/var
canonicalisation doesn't false-positive.

This is a partial fix. Shell tools can still escape via `cd /other &&
write-stuff` — that needs OS-level sandbox enforcement (bwrap /
sandbox-exec) and is explicitly not addressed here.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
